text:
Two Claude Code hook events feed two different assembly functions, both funneling through the same renderer:

| Hook event | Daemon RPC | Assembly fn | Cursor semantics |
|---|---|---|---|
| `SessionStart` | `session_start` | registers session only, no fabric text | — |
| `UserPromptSubmit` | `turn_start` | `assemble_turn_start_context` (`src/turn_context/start.rs:23`) | `cursor = rec.seen_cursor` (0 on first turn → full render); advances `seen_cursor` to `now` after rendering |
| `PostToolUse` | `turn_check` | `assemble_turn_check_context` (`src/turn_context/check.rs:7`) | `cursor = delta_since` from a CAS-advanced `seen_cursor`; `None` (→ nothing rendered) if the CAS lost a race |

Hook → RPC mapping is registered in `src/cli/install/config.rs:87-93` (`claude_hook_entries`) and dispatched in `src/cli/hooks.rs:277-424` (`hook_dispatch`). Output is emitted as plain text on stdout for `UserPromptSubmit` and as a `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` JSON envelope for `PostToolUse` (`src/cli/turn.rs:129-146`, `src/cli/hooks.rs:183-195`).
